findings.evidence: State-level general laws already exist at lines 96, 103, and 109; the diagram namespace only has identity laws at lines 271/276 and swap involutions at lines 314/320.
findings.fix: Add diagram-level analogues `relabelRows_relabelRows`, `relabelColumns_relabelColumns`, and `relabelRows_relabelColumns`, then derive the swap involution lemmas from those general statements.
findings.issue: `GridDiagram.swapRows_swapRows` and `swapColumns_swapColumns` are special-case diagram proofs, while `GridDiagram.relabelRows`/`relabelColumns` have no diagram-level composition or row-column commutation lemmas.

summary: The state-level relabeling API is at the right generality, but the diagram-level API drops back to swap-only corollaries instead of exposing the same general action laws.
